title: VOA — Kitchen-Table Politics, Yosemite, ADHD Awareness, Pearl S. Buck
content: Section 1: types of politics idioms · Section 2: Yosemite National Park · Section 3: rise in adult ADHD diagnoses · Section 4: Pearl S. Buck — first American woman to win the Nobel in Literature
attribution: Audio courtesy of VOA Learning English (learningenglish.voanews.com) — public domain. Questions © IELTS Band AI.
license: public domain (US federal government work, commercial reuse permitted with attribution)

questions.question: Kitchen-table politics is sometimes also called ________-table politics.
Another common synonym is 'bread-and-________ issues'.
Which of the following is NOT given as a typical kitchen-table issue?
options: A. The price of a foreign holiday
questions.answer: A. The price of a foreign holiday

questions.question: Merriam-Webster defines 'bread-and-butter' as 'as basic as the earning of one's ________'.
What does 'green politics' mainly focus on?
Three issues central to green politics are environmental sustainability, ________, and threats to wildlife.
________ politics is shaped by personal characteristics such as gender, age, and religion.
Which type of politics centres on damaging opponents through public accusations of wrongdoing?
The word 'gotcha' is short for the phrase 'I ________ you'.
Cambridge Dictionary defines a 'bread-and-butter issue' as:
options: A. A subject related to most people's needs and interests
B. A government tax on imported food goods
C. A legal punishment imposed after wrongdoing
D. A new technology that spreads very quickly
questions.answer: A. A subject related to most people's needs and interests

questions.question: Yosemite National Park is high in the ________ Nevada Mountains of California.
The park lies about ________ kilometres east of San Francisco.
How many waterfalls does Yosemite Valley contain?
Yosemite Falls is described as the ________ highest waterfall on Earth.
The granite formation called Half Dome rises more than ________ metres into the air.
Roughly how old is the giant Sequoia known as 'Grizzly Giant'?
The first people to live in the area were the ________ tribe.
In 1864, President ________ signed a bill granting Yosemite Valley to California as a public park.
In what year did Yosemite officially become a national park?
Approximately how many people visit Yosemite every year?
More than ________ million U.S. adults have been diagnosed with ADHD.
That figure is roughly equivalent to:
More than ________ million U.S. children have also been diagnosed with ADHD.
About what share of adults with ADHD were first diagnosed at age 18 or later?
The patient profiled in the report, Allison Burk, was diagnosed at age:
Allison Burk lives in Columbus, ________.
According to Ohio State psychologist Justin Barterian, ADHD-assessment requests have:
options: A. Doubled in the past two years
B. Halved in the past two years
D. Risen by about five percent
questions.answer: A. Doubled in the past two years

questions.question: Diagnostic criteria for ADHD were broadened in the year ________.
Which body is preparing new adult-diagnosis guidelines?
options: A. The American Professional Society of ADHD and Related Disorders
B. The U.S. Food and Drug Administration
questions.answer: A. The American Professional Society of ADHD and Related Disorders

questions.question: How is ADHD diagnosed today?
options: A. Through history and behavioural assessments — no blood or brain test exists
B. Through a single routine blood test
C. Through a brain MRI scan
D. Through a genetic saliva test
questions.answer: A. Through history and behavioural assessments — no blood or brain test exists

questions.question: Pearl S. Buck's best-known novel, ________, was published in 1931.
She won the Pulitzer Prize for fiction in the year ________.
In 1938 she became the first American woman to win:
options: A. The Nobel Prize in Literature
B. The Pulitzer Prize for fiction
questions.answer: A. The Nobel Prize in Literature

questions.question: Across her career she wrote more than ________ books.
She spent nearly ________ years of her life in China.
What subject did she study at Randolph-Macon Woman's College in Virginia?
Her husband, John Lossing Buck, was a religious worker who studied ________.
The two main characters in The Good Earth are Chinese farmer Wang Lung and his wife ________.
Pearl S. Buck's final home, Green Hills Farm, was located in:
She died in 1973 at the age of:
